title: nvm_list ignores out_count; may make excess round-trips
description: wh_Client_NvmList returns out_count on each call. The current nvm_list loop calls the API once per object, advancing start_id by 1 each iteration and ignoring out_count entirely. If the wolfHSM server can return multiple IDs per call (batch listing), ignoring out_count forces O(N) round-trips instead of O(N/batch) round-trips to enumerate N NVM objects. Expected impact: on a device with many NVM objects (counters, certs, keys) this is linear in N; each round-trip has non-trivial latency over IPC transports. Needs verification against the C API spec to confirm whether out_count > 1 is ever meaningful, but the current code is provably sub-optimal if it is.
